reasoning: The US recently implemented major export control regulations in January 2025 covering AI chips and model weights. Assuming standard administrative momentum, another major overhaul within the same calendar year would normally be unlikely (base rate of ~1 per 12-18 months). However, the specific omission of cloud access controls—despite an existing proposed rule (IaaS) and ongoing discussions to close this known "loophole"—makes an additional targeted regulation quite plausible before the year's end. The change of administration in 2025 may also act as a catalyst for tighter or expanded controls. Balancing the likelihood of bureaucratic delay against strong geopolitical motivations, I assign a 45% probability.
